title: Airbnb Q3 2025 Financial Results
text: Airbnb reported Q3 results with Gross Booking Value increased 14 percent year-over-year, while Nights and Seats Booked rose by nine percent, with both metrics accelerating from Q2 and exceeding expectations. Revenue increased 10 percent year-over-year in Q3 2025, landing at the high end of the company's guidance range. Adjusted EBITDA was over $2.0 billion in Q3 2025, the company's highest in any quarter. The company runs a roughly 35% adjusted EBITDA margin while still investing heavily in product. Free cash flow continued to be strong at over 38% on a trailing 12-month basis. The balance sheet holds $10.6 billion in liquidity against just $2 billion in debt. A $6 billion buyback authorization signals management confidence. Strategic initiatives include Hotel expansion beyond initial pilots, with boutique and independent properties being prioritized. Services and Experiences offerings have been significantly expanded. AI implementation across customer service and product discovery continues with plans for voice capabilities. The World Cup preparation infrastructure is underway for expected largest event in company history by guest count.
